text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy village nestled among ancient forests, an eccentric botanist named Dr. Amelia Lotus lived in a cozy one-bedroom cottage. She was renowned not only for her extraordinary flower collections but also for her whimsical tales. Despite her isolated life in the woods, Dr. Lotus kept an eye out for the strangest beings.

One fine afternoon, a peculiar feather boa floated down from the leafy branches. Excitedly, Dr. Lotus scooped it up, murmuring to herself about the elusive quetzallow they’d heard whispering tales. That night, under a full moon, she imagined a creature wearing that feather—one she hadn't seen since spring.

Dr. Lotus dreamed she heard a soft, melodic song. She followed its tune, guided by the glimmering feathers that danced around her feet. When dawn broke, she discovered a forest clearing bathed in a gentle twilight. There, perched on a large fir tree, was a bird unlike
